channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Matt Gaetz meets privately with senators to shore up support as House Ethics decision looms
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump’s pick for attorney general, Matt Gaetz, has launched an effort to personally secure his embattled nomination.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Mascaro And Stephen Groves, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Sprint to confirm Trump nominees kicks off in January
item.description: How many? And how fast? That’s the question for the Senate in January as it will sprint to confirm as many of President-elect Donald Trump’s nominees as possible.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump's immigration grand strategy casts wide net
item.description: President-elect Trump is returning to the White House looking to dwarf his first term's significant impact on immigration policy.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Battle for DNC chair kicks off under shadow of Trump
item.description: The battle over who will take the reins of the Democratic National Committee (DNC) has begun as the party points fingers and works to refocus after resounding losses on Election Day.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: Congressional election observers deploy to Iowa for recount in uncalled House race
item.description: Congressional election observers deployed to Iowa after Democrat Bohannan called a recount in the House race against GOP incumbent Miller-Meeks.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden makes another Ukraine policy shift with approval of sending anti-personnel mines
item.description: Ukrainian forces are now being supplied with American anti-personnel mines, a second escalation by President Biden before leaving office.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump Cabinet picks give some of his Muslim voters pause
item.description: Muslims for Trump co-founder Rabiul Chowdhury said some of the nominations are concerning for voters.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: What to know about Linda McMahon, Trump’s pick for Education secretary
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Linda McMahon has been a constant presence in Donald Trump's tumultuous orbit, serving in his first administration and supporting his presidential campaigns. Now he's chosen her to serve as Education secretary. Here's a look at McMahon's background, from business to politics.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Someone accessed files said to contain damaging info about Trump AG-nominee Gaetz, lawyer says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — An unauthorized person gained access to a file containing confidential testimony from women who have made allegations about former Rep. Matt Gaetz, Donald Trump's pick to become the next attorney general, a lawyer said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: VP-elect JD Vance to hold meetings between Gaetz, Hegseth and 'key' GOP senators
item.description: A source told Fox News Digital VP-elect JD Vance will schedule meetings with key GOP senators and Trump Cabinet nominees Matt Gaetz and Pete Hegseth on Capitol Hill.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Minimum wage isn't going up: California votes against Prop 32
item.description: Proposition 32 would have raised the minimum wage for any business with 26 or more employees to $17 an hour immediately -- and raise it once more to $18 an hour starting January 1, 2025.
item.dc:creator: Dom McAndrew, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: California voters reject measure that would have raised minimum wage to nation-high $18 per hour
item.description: California voters have rejected a measure on the November ballot that would have raised the minimum wage for most workers to $18 an hour.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump interviewing candidates for FBI chief, Vance says in later-deleted social media post
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump is interviewing candidates for the role of FBI director, incoming Vice President JD Vance said Tuesday in the clearest indication yet that the new administration is looking to replace current director Christopher Wray.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump watches as Musk’s SpaceX launches Starship rocket
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump is expected to attend the launch of a SpaceX rocket in Texas on Tuesday, sources familiar with the plans confirmed, in the latest sign of Trump’s closeness to the company's billionaire CEO Elon Musk.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Jeffries: Republicans don't have a mandate
item.description: House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries (D-N.Y.) on Tuesday rejected the idea that voters delivered an Election Day mandate to Donald Trump and the Republicans, arguing that the GOP’s razor-thin majority in the House means that nothing will get done in Washington without Democratic support.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, NewsNation

item.title: House Republicans signal support for proposal to ban bathroom access for 1st transgender member
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — House Speaker Mike Johnson signaled support Tuesday for a Republican effort to ban Democrat Sarah McBride — the first transgender person to be elected to Congress — from using women’s restrooms in the Capitol once she's sworn into office next year.
item.dc:creator: FARNOUSH AMIRI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Prosecutors oppose tossing Trump’s hush money conviction but are open to sentencing delay
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York prosecutors oppose any effort to dismiss President-elect Donald Trump’s hush money conviction, but they expressed openness Tuesday to delaying sentencing until after his second term.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK and JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US-Israel panel to look into civilian harm in Gaza is set for first meeting next month
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Biden administration says a U.S.-Israel panel to look into reports of civilian harm from the war in Gaza will meet for the first time in early December, missing by more than a month a U.S. call for the channel to be set up by the end of October.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Donald Trump to nominate Dr. Oz for Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump plans to nominate former Pennsylvania Senate nominee Dr. Mehmet Oz to serve as the Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services (CMS) Administrator.
item.dc:creator: George Stockburger, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US will send Ukraine at least $275 million in new weapons in push to bolster Kyiv before Trump
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Pentagon will send Ukraine at least $275 million in new weapons, U.S. officials said Tuesday, as the Biden administration rushes to do as much as it can to help Kyiv fight back against Russia before President-elect Donald Trump takes office.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR and MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump chooses TV doctor Mehmet Oz to lead Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump says he is nominating Dr. Mehmet Oz, who hosted a long-running television talk show, to lead the Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Hakeem Jeffries wins reelection as House Democratic leader despite party's losses
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — House Leader Hakeem Jeffries won reelection Tuesday as the Democratic leader, receiving support from his colleagues despite the party's inability to win back majority control of the chamber in the November election.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A GOP lawmaker introduces a Capitol bathroom rule before her trans colleague arrives
item.description: South Carolina Rep. Nancy Mace says the resolution aims to protect women's rights. Democratic Rep.-elect Sarah McBride, the first out trans person elected to Congress, has slammed it as a distraction.
item.dc:creator: Elena Moore and Rachel Treisman, MPR News

item.title: Trump expected to pick Howard Lutnick for Commerce secretary
item.description: President-elect Trump is expected to nominate Howard Lutnick to serve as Commerce secretary, a source familiar told The Hill. Lutnick is the chair and CEO of Cantor Fitzgerald and is also currently serving as co-chair of the Trump transition.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Trump chooses billionaire and key transition adviser Howard Lutnick for commerce secretary
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump has chosen Howard Lutnick, head of brokerage and investment bank Cantor Fitzgerald and cryptocurrency enthusiast, as his nominee for commerce secretary.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Hurricane debate at state capitol heats up as temperatures cool down
item.description: Cooler temperatures mean hurricane season is almost behind us, but the debate in the state capital over storm recovery is just heating up.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 2 women tell Ethics panel Matt Gaetz paid them for sex, attorney says
item.description: Leppard said the House Ethics Committee and his clients went one by one through a series of Venmo transactions.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Georgia appeals court cancels arguments in Trump's bid to disqualify Fani Willis
item.description: A Georgia appeals court canceled next month’s oral arguments in President-elect Trump’s bid to disqualify Fulton County District Attorney Fani Willis (D) from criminally prosecuting him. Monday's one-sentence order from the Georgia Court of Appeals canceling the Dec.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Size of slim Republican House majority hangs on 5 uncalled races
item.description: There are still five uncalled House races that will determine the size of the Republican majority in Congress after the 2024 elections.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Accused of violating worker rights, SpaceX and Amazon go after labor board
item.description: SpaceX and Amazon are asking the Fifth Circuit Court of Appeals to find the National Labor Relations Board unconstitutional. The federal agency is tasked with enforcing workers' right to organize.
item.dc:creator: Andrea Hsu, MPR News

item.title: Trump taps FCC's Brendan Carr to lead the agency
item.description: Carr was seen as a pretty conventional Republican with a pro-corporate outlook for most of his career. More recently, he has embraced Trumpian themes about social media, tech and television companies.
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik, MPR News

item.title: Whoopi Goldberg called to apologize for saying bakery denied order over politics
item.description: New York City politicians are calling on Whoopi Goldberg to apologize for suggesting on air that a local bakery had declined to make “The View” co-host a batch of desserts for her birthday because of her political beliefs.
item.dc:creator: Philip Marcelo, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Former Detroit Lions player charged over Capitol riot
item.description: A former Detroit Lions linebacker was arrested and charged with allegedly assaulting law enforcement and other offenses over the Jan. 6, 2021, Capitol riot, according to the Department of Justice (DOJ).
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Businesses hope Trump administration knows value of migrants to US economy
item.description: As the Trump administration prepares to take back the White House, several business and migrant advocacy groups on Thursday said they hope the incoming administration realizes the economic contributions that migrants and asylum-seekers make to the U.S. economy.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, Fox 5

item.title: What can Florida expect from the next legislative session
item.description: Florida legislature meets for an organization session 14 days after the general election to swear in new members and speak on what the year ahead could look like at the capitol.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Thousands of votes never counted across Nevada due to signature issues
item.description: More than 9,000 mail-in ballots across Nevada were not counted because voters did not verify their signatures before the curing deadline, according to data the 8 News Now Investigators reviewed.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Cannizzaro, Yeager to continue leadership roles in Nevada Senate, Assembly
item.description: Democratic Nevada State Senator Nicole Cannizzaro will serve for a fourth consecutive legislative session as Senate Majority Leader. Democrat Assembly Speaker Steve Yeager was also reelected.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Bleakley, KLAS, CBS News
